text: This is Dr. Craig Rogers, Director of Renal Surgery at the Vatakuta Urology Institute at Henry Ford Hospital. In this video, we demonstrate reconstruction of the inner layer of the resection bed.

The robotic instruments are exchanged for a robotic needle driver, while the ProGrasp or fenestrated bipolar instruments remain in the left hand, potentially used as a needle driver depending on the surgeon's preference.

The surgeon uses a 3-0 monocryl suture on an SH needle to reconstruct the inner layer of the resection bed. The suture is passed through the renal capsule into the defect, and anchored with a knot and a hemlock clip on the renal capsule.

The surgeon uses continuous suturing to close small vessels and repair entries into the collecting system within the resection bed. Various suture options, including forovicral and barb sutures, are available depending on the surgeon's preference.
Cure must be taken not to place sutures too deep to avoid vascular injury, excluding the collecting system or pseudoaneurysm.

The surgeon passes the needle from the inner layer to the outside of the kidney through the renal capsule, securing the stitch with a hemlock clip placed by the assistant. Additional sutures are applied to occlude small bleeding vessels.

The surgeon focuses on reconstructing the inner layer of the kidney using sutures to achieve hemostasis during renorraphy, ensuring the closure of small vessels and repair of any entries into the collecting system.
In the next video, we will demonstrate the technique for sutured closure of the renal capsule.
